etymology.description: 犬(犬) (quǎn, "dog") eating ⺼ (肉) (ròu, "meat") but 口 corrupted to 甘 then 日 -> satisfied
etymology.interpretation: The character originally depicted a dog eating meat, conveying the idea of being satisfied. Over time, the component 口, which represented the mouth, evolved into 甘 and later 日 in the modern form, altering the visual etymology while retaining the sense of satiation.
